g.data-narration: Iteration and Calibration are fundamental numerical methods for solving linear and nonlinear equations in engineering analysis and scientific computing applications.
g.text: Iteration and Calibration in Numerical Methods

g.data-narration: The Jacobi method decomposes matrix A into diagonal D and off-diagonal L+U parts, computing all new values simultaneously from previous iteration values.
g.text: Jacobi Iteration Method
Formula: x^(k+1) = D⁻¹(b - (L+U)x^(k))
Decomposes A = D + L + U, computes all variables simultaneously
Converges if matrix is strictly diagonally dominant or symmetric positive definite

g.data-narration: Gauss-Seidel improves upon Jacobi by using updated values immediately as they become available, typically converging twice as fast for most practical engineering problems.
g.text: Gauss-Seidel Iteration Method
Formula: x^(k+1) = D⁻¹(b - Lx^(k+1) - Ux^(k))
Uses updated values immediately, faster convergence than Jacobi
Convergence: same conditions as Jacobi method required

g.data-narration: SOR method adds a relaxation parameter omega to accelerate convergence. Omega equals one gives Gauss-Seidel, optimal omega typically ranges from one to two.
g.text: Successive Over-Relaxation (SOR) Method
Formula: x^(k+1) = (D+ωL)⁻¹[ωb - ωUx^(k) + (1-ω)Dx^(k)]
Relaxation parameter ω accelerates convergence when properly chosen
For convergence: 0 < ω < 2 required; ω = 1 gives Gauss-Seidel

g.data-narration: Newton-Raphson solves nonlinear equations by linearizing at each iteration using the derivative, achieving quadratic convergence near the root when conditions are favorable.
g.text: Newton-Raphson Method
Formula: x^(k+1) = x^(k) - f(x^(k))/f'(x^(k))
For systems: x^(k+1) = x^(k) - J⁻¹f(x^(k)) where J is Jacobian matrix
Requires good initial guess and f'(x^k) ≠ 0; achieves quadratic convergence

g.data-narration: Convergence criteria check residual magnitude or displacement change between iterations. Common tests include absolute residual, relative residual, and displacement norms for accurate solutions.
g.text: Convergence Criteria
Absolute residual: ||r^(k)|| < ε₁
Relative residual: ||r^(k)||/||r^(0)|| < ε₂
Displacement criterion: ||x^(k+1) - x^(k)|| < ε₃

g.data-narration: Calibration adjusts model parameters to match experimental data. The h-method reduces element size while p-method increases polynomial order for mesh refinement.
g.text: Calibration and Mesh Refinement
Calibration: Adjusts parameters to minimize difference between predictions and experimental data
h-method: Reduces element size h while keeping polynomial degree constant
p-method: Increases polynomial order p while keeping mesh fixed
hp-method: Combines both approaches for exponential convergence
